description: Remove existing static files and scaffold a new Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase client in the repository root.
steps: Remove existing files: index.html, css/, js/, images/ directories

Run 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --import-alias @/* --use-npm --no-git' to scaffold the Next.js project in the current directory. Use --no-git to avoid reinitializing git.
Install Supabase client: 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npm install @supabase/supabase-js'
Create .env.local with placeholder Supabase credentials (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY) and admin credentials (ADMIN_USERNAME, ADMIN_PASSWORD)
Create .env.example with the same keys but empty values for documentation
Create src/lib/supabase.ts with Supabase client initialization using createClient from @supabase/supabase-js

Create supabase-schema.sql with the database schema: albums table (id uuid primary key, name text, slug text unique, cover_photo_url text, description text, created_at timestamp) and photos table (id uuid primary key, album_id uuid references albums, url text, caption text, storage_path text, created_at timestamp). Include Supabase Storage bucket creation for 'photos'.
Verify the project builds: 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npm run build'
acceptance_criteria: Old static files (index.html, css/, js/, images/) are removed
Next.js project scaffolded with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
src/lib/supabase.ts exports a configured Supabase client
supabase-schema.sql contains valid SQL for albums and photos tables
Running 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npm run build' completes successfully
